concept.core_loop: Turn-based arcane duel: each turn draw 5 spell cards, spend 3 mana to cast fire/ice/lightning cards against a telegraphed enemy, then end turn and the enemy acts. The live decision each turn is solving the enemy's telegraphed intent — set up Burn/Chill with fire/ice, then cash it in with lightning for bonus damage, while budgeting block vs offense. Between fights, pick 1 of 3 new cards to deepen the deck. Difficulty ramps across a ~5-node run (combat, combat, rest, elite+relic, boss). One excellent run, not endless.
mechanics: meta save: unlock 1 card + ascension toggle on boss win
concept.art_direction: Arcane-sigil SVG vector: deep indigo/violet base; element-accented spell cards (fire amber/orange, ice cyan/white, lightning electric-yellow/violet); runic circles, mana crystals, glowing sigils, glyph-bordered card frames with a cost gem and per-element sigil; a wide arcane-chamber background (indigo->violet gradient, glowing floor runic circle, floating motes) composed for the 1280x720 frame; distinct enemy silhouettes (imp, frost-wraith, golem, boss archmage). Feedback: cards arc to center on cast, number pop-ups, enemy hit-flash + screen shake scaled to damage, status icons with stack counts, enemy death dissolve.
concept.differentiation_notes: A tight, hand-crafted vertical-slice deckbuilder built around one discoverable hook — fire/ice set up a status, lightning cashes it in — rather than a wide card pool. Landscape-native (the project's first non-portrait title), so the fanned hand and card frames read large.

theme.premise: a lone mage's arcane duel through a sequence of monsters, casting elemental spell cards
theme.setting: a candle-lit arcane duelling chamber ringed with glowing runes

visual_system.world_bible: One candle-lit arcane duelling chamber of indigo-violet stone ringed with glowing runes. Every actor is a SUMMONED entity of that one world — a violet-stone silhouette (gradient body #46306a->#281a44, lighter rune-stroke outline) with glowing element-tinted rune-eyes and a soft element-tinted aura. The four enemies are one family read by silhouette+element: imp (small, horned, bat-winged, fire/amber), frost_wraith (tall floating hooded shroud, ice/cyan), golem (broad blocky construct with a glowing core, stone/violet), archmage boss (robed sorcerer, pointed hat, rune-orb staff, halo of runes, lightning/violet). Spell cards are glyph-bordered stone-and-rune scrolls cut from the same material, element-tinted to match the spell.
visual_system.stroke: ~2-3px lighter-tint outline, round join/cap, plus an outer low-alpha element-tinted glow halo on every framed/aura element
visual_system.form: rounded-rect glyph-bordered card frames (corner radius) + organic creature silhouettes, each with a signature: imp horns+wings+barbed tail, wraith tattered hooded shroud, golem blocky core-rune, archmage hat+staff+rune halo; per-element card sigil (flame / 6-fold snowflake / bolt-in-ring / 8-point star rune)
visual_system.shading: flat fill + single vertical body gradient + outer glow halo (element-tinted) — one model everywhere
visual_system.scale: cards authored at 2x footprint (240x320 viewBox -> drawn 120x160) for high-DPI headroom; enemies 240x240 viewBox -> drawn 220px centered at the enemy anchor; background 1280x720 1:1 full-frame

reskinned: card_frame:fire/ice/lightning/neutral (4 element frames, drawn behind the dynamic cost-gem + name/type/effect text)
enemy:imp/frost_wraith/golem/archmage (4 distinct silhouettes, flash=overbright modulate pass, enrage=scale-pop, dissolve=alpha)
left_primitive: all juice/motion (screen shake, card-cast arc ghost, number pop-ups, death dissolve+particles, HP/mana tweens, status pulses, intent telegraph) — motion not art
HUD chrome (HP/mana/block panel, pile counts, End Turn button, reward/win/lose overlays) — functional UI text/rects
cost gem + enemy HP bar/intent/status-icon badges — dynamic data drawn over the frame/silhouette
asset_pass.notes: draw_texture*-in-place strategy (preferred for the builder default single-_draw immediate-mode renderer): textures load once in _ready, replace the primitive draw calls in _draw_background/_draw_enemy/_draw_card_face, preserving z-order + shake + all juice for free. Each draw helper guards on a null texture and falls back to the original primitive so a missing import never hard-fails. SVG is the correct method here — concept.art_direction is explicitly arcane-SIGIL VECTOR (geometric frames/runes/silhouettes), resolution-independent across Android density buckets. Hit-flash on a textured silhouette done via an overbright (modulate>1) additive second draw_texture_rect pass — no shader needed. All 4 enemies + 4 card frames + bg verified in-frame on the RTX 5080 real renderer; selftest still SELFTEST OK (rules untouched).
